title: Vertex Pharmaceuticals and CRISPR Therapeutics Amend Collaboration for CTX001 Development and Commercialization
text: Vertex and CRISPR Therapeutics Collaboration Amendment. Primary Collaboration for CTX001 (CASGEVY): Vertex Pharmaceuticals and CRISPR Therapeutics amended their collaboration agreement to develop, manufacture and commercialize CTX001, an investigational CRISPR/Cas9-based gene editing therapy being developed as a potentially curative therapy for sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia. Key Terms of the Amended Agreement: Leadership and Responsibilities: Vertex will lead global development, manufacturing and commercialization of CTX001 with support from CRISPR Therapeutics. Cost and Profit Sharing: Vertex will be responsible for 60% of program costs and will receive 60% of profits from future sales of CTX001 worldwide, representing a 10% increase in program economics compared to the previous agreement. CRISPR will be responsible for 40% of costs and will receive 40% of profits. Financial Payments: CRISPR will receive a $900 million upfront payment, with potential for a $200 million payment upon the first regulatory approval of CTX001. Separate Type 1 Diabetes Licensing Agreement: Vertex and CRISPR Therapeutics entered into a new non-exclusive licensing agreement for the use of CRISPR Therapeutics' gene editing technology for hypoimmune cell therapies for type 1 diabetes. T1D Agreement Terms: Vertex will pay CRISPR Therapeutics $100 million up-front for non-exclusive rights, with eligibility for up to an additional $230 million in research and development milestones and royalties on any future products.
